name: Sticky Scroll Reveal
summary: A scroll-driven component that displays content items sequentially with a sticky preview panel and color-changing backgrounds.
detailedDescription: Sticky Scroll Reveal creates an interactive scroll experience where content items are revealed sequentially as the user scrolls. The component uses Framer Motion's useScroll hook to track scroll progress within a scrollable container, mapping scroll position to active card indices. As users scroll, the component calculates which content item is closest based on breakpoint positions (dividing scroll range into equal segments per item). The active item triggers two visual changes: the background color cycles through a predefined array of dark colors (slate-900, black, neutral-900), and a sticky preview panel on the right displays the active item's content. Text content (titles and descriptions) fades in/out based on active state - active items show at full opacity while inactive items dim to 30%. The preview panel uses a sticky position to remain visible while scrolling, with a configurable gradient background that also cycles through an array based on active index. This creates an engaging storytelling experience perfect for feature showcases, product highlights, or step-by-step narratives.
purpose: Display feature highlights with sticky visual previews
Guide users through sequential content narratives
behavior: Tracks scroll progress using Framer Motion useScroll
Calculates active item based on scroll position breakpoints
Cycles background colors through predefined array
Updates sticky preview panel content on active change
Fades text opacity based on active/inactive state
Uses sticky positioning for persistent preview panel
visualCharacteristics: Scrollable left column with text content
Sticky right panel showing active content preview
Background color shifts between dark tones
Active items at full opacity, inactive at 30%
recommendations: Use where moderate motion and reusable composition improve UX and clarity.
